name: Press Room: Dean Browning for Congress
subject: Reminder: Republican Dean Browning Rolls Out Early Endorsements for 2020 Congressional Race in PA-7
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BROWNING FOR CONGRESS
body:
Conservative businessman and proud Trump Republican says incumbent Rep. Susan Wild is far too liberal for Pennsylvania’s 7th Congressional District
---
| | | | | |
---
| Republican Dean Browning Rolls Out Early Endorsements for 2020 Congressional Race in PA-7
---
| Conservative businessman and proud Trump Republican says incumbent Rep. Susan Wild is far too liberal for Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District
---
| For Immediate Release Contact: Dean Browning (484-550-6550)
Allentown, Friday, June 14, 2019 - Conservative businessman and Trump Republican Dean Browning rolled out an impressive list of early endorsements today, just shy of a year before the 2020 primary in Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District.
In 2018, Browning's insurgent, grassroots campaign fell short by less than one percentage point against Marty Nothstein in the Republican primary.
"I am honored to receive the early endorsements of so many trusted conservative leaders across the 7th Congressional District in support of my campaign to make Susan Wild a one-term Congresswoman," said Browning. "During her first 6+ months in office, Rep. Susan Wild has proven herself far too liberal to represent the Lehigh Valley in Congress. We must make sure that she faces a strong challenge when she seeks re-election in 2020. My pro-Trump, pro-2nd Amendment and pro-life candidacy offers the clear and compelling contrast Republicans need against Susan Wild and the increasingly socialist Democrat Party in Washington."
Dean Browning's Announced Endorsements
Justin Simmons, State Representative Glenn Eckhart, Lehigh County Controller Nate Brown, Lehigh County Commissioner Matt Dietz, Northampton County Councilman Brent Labenberg, Emmaus Borough Councilman David Bond, South Whitehall Township Commissioner Phyllis Andrews, Bath Borough Councilwoman Robert Smith, Allentown School Board Director Sean Gill, Upper Macungie Township Supervisor William Heydt, Former Allentown Mayor Roger Reis, Former Lehigh County Commissioner Andy Roman, Former Lehigh County Commissioner Hayden Phillips, Former Northampton County Councilman Pat Breslin, Former Bethlehem Township Commissioner Lou Hershman, Former Allentown City Councilman Scott Armstrong, Former Allentown School Board Director Tom Campione, Past President, Lehigh Valley Tea Party Ronnie DelBacco, Past President, Lehigh Valley Tea Party
[Browning's platform](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oRa3RHnAoNrK4nh8gahpc1RU2dO9fdg4CwgIlVQKZ6-ONtPlyRHld9z3f2m9INf-F3LHZvsHffHB2BHPpAht28funwjKxBKx3kOm3eITeblWntkHvXYEhzfX5-9VaA4zA-MyDrR8KpgT1uOuA_D3SQRDA6RI52v2EXsNitFlXr4MIZURoY6MrQ==&c=iA5rphc8zzGJ1wGAzo7l8NiOxZqz9AAIWR6hyOJvQTwXSd93EDBS-A==&ch=HVFFn3ni2OJs4nt8YK7P-U7a6wWizSfsiwz1-vl_sMrPbCwPvnJEmA==) includes building the wall on the US-Mexico border, strengthening our military, an America first economic policy, defending the 2nd Amendment and protecting the unborn.

"I am honored to receive the early endorsements of so many trusted conservative leaders across the 7th Congressional District in support of my campaign to make Susan Wild a one-term Congresswoman," said Browning. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Bahrain Workshop on Palestinian Economy is the Opportunity of a Generation
body:
"This is exciting -- an opportunity of a generation. We are grateful to the Kingdom of Bahrain for extending the invitation to..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ef3b7b461c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Bahrain Workshop on Palestinian Economy is the Opportunity of a Generation**

“This is exciting -- an opportunity of a generation. We are grateful to the Kingdom of Bahrain for extending the invitation to [the Trump Administration] to host the June 25 and 26 workshop with them for the benefit of Palestinians and others in the region, offering us a unique opportunity to communicate our economic vision,” White House Special Representative for International Negotiations Jason Greenblatt writes in CNN.

“This workshop will facilitate discussions on an ambitious, achievable vision and framework for a prosperous future for Palestinians . . . President Trump is informed by previous attempts at peace but not encumbered by them. He is seeking a realistic resolution and a brighter future for all those burdened with this conflict.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f16b460592&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration is doing just about everything it can to slow the flood of undocumented Mexicans and Central Americans coming to the U.S. and claiming asylum. But alleviating our growing border crisis is impossible unless Congress changes our immigration laws,” the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e89b57ed20&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. “It is certainly true that most Democrats are at best muddled on the immigration question and at worst fully in favor of opening the southern border to anyone who wants to come into the country. But the party of the Left cannot deny that we have a crisis at the southern border.”
---
|
---
| “Some Border Patrol agents in Texas are concerned about exposure to Ebola by a migrant fleeing the Democratic Republic of the Congo for the United States. But more of them are worried about other illnesses frequently popping up among detainees at stations across the southern border,” Anna Giaritelli reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a73fdd6ccf&e=3ee4985eea). “Border Patrol’s holding facilities in the Del Rio and El Paso sectors, or regions, are inundated with sick detainees, as well as sick agents.”
---
|
---
| “U.S. factory production bounced back in May after a four-month slump while the growth of industrial output in China fell to its slowest pace since 2002,” John Carney reports for [_Breitbart_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8c5861b4e0&e=3ee4985eea). “U.S. tariffs are taking a toll on the world’s second-largest economy. The U.S. factory data is an important sign that the U.S. manufacturing sector is more resilient to trade stress than many economists had forecast.”
---
|
---
| “Now [President Trump] is the namesake of a tiny Israeli settlement in the Israel-controlled Golan Heights,” Ilan Ben Zion writes in [_**The Associated Press**_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=acd729381d&e=3ee4985eea). “Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s Cabinet convened in this hamlet Sunday to inaugurate a new settlement named after President Donald Trump in a gesture of appreciation for the U.S. leader’s recognition of Israeli sovereignty over the territory.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5f8571ecc7&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=600f7748a9&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=4777598cc4)
---

---

name: Cory Booker
subject: The most important two weeks of this election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, At the end of this email, I'll ask you for a donation to our campaign, but I want to tell you exactly why first: **The next two weeks are the most important we 've faced in this election.** Next Wednesday, I take the stage in the first Democratic debate. It'll be our first chance to speak directly to voters across the country on equal footing with the other candidates in this race. No matter what the polls say, no matter how much money a campaign has raised, each of us will have our time in the spotlight. I can't wait. **There 's something else happening just a few days after that debate that you need to know about.** After June ends, each campaign is required to file a finance report with the FEC. It's the first fundraising update since early spring, which means people will read into these numbers, making big conclusions about which campaigns have momentum, and which campaigns don't -- especially after everyone picks apart the first debate. But if you look a little deeper, these reports provide a window into how candidates are funding their campaigns. For instance, it's a whole lot easier to collect big checks from corporate PACs and D.C. lobbyists, but we refuse to play that game. When we file our FEC report I'm proud to say that every single donation will represent somebody chipping in not to try to win influence or power -- but because they share a bold and unapologetically optimistic vision for what we can do together. That's why the names on our report will always mean so much more to me than the dollars donated. You will always be the people who took a chance on me and this underdog campaign from the start. In spite of the odds stacked against us, you believe in this campaign and you're giving me a fighting chance to be our party's nominee. In this field of amazing Democrats, I know it's no small choice to make a donation to one campaign. I can't tell you how honored I am by every single person who decides to help build this. **[So I'm asking, humbly: Will you make a donation today and add one more grassroots supporter to our FEC report?](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190617-em?refcode=em_fr_20190617_CB_20190617-fr-CB-eoqlaunch-actives_2020ndnr_longl1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Person by person, brick by brick, we're building something incredible. And I'm so grateful you're a part of it. Cory
[](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190617-em?refcode=em_fr_20190617_CB_20190617-fr-CB-eoqlaunch-actives_actives_longcontribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")
---
| | Paid for by Cory 2020
---

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09ed/5fca0d19/7986b13/291b8f08/1721924683/VEsD/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09ed/5fca0d19/7986b13/291b8f17/1721924683/VEsA/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09ed/5fca0d19/7986b13/291b8f16/1721924683/VEsB/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I am asking some very simple questions that are too rarely asked
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1729%2E150750%2Ex9hSQK)
Sisters and Brothers -
During the last 45 years there have been a lot of campaign promises made by candidates, a lot of political party platforms, a lot of great speeches and a lot of important pieces of legislation passed.
But, at the end of all of that, we have to ask ourselves some very simple questions that are too rarely asked.
Why, despite huge increases in technology and worker productivity, is the average American worker today – in real, inflation-accounted-for dollars – earning no more than he or she did in 1974?
Why are half of our people living paycheck to paycheck, with no money in the bank and scared to death that their car might break down or that a family member could end up in the hospital?
Why, in the last 30 years, has there been a massive transfer of wealth from the working families of this country to the very rich – with the top 1% seeing its wealth increase by $21 trillion – while the bottom half saw a $900 billion decline in its wealth?
Why, for the first time in the modern history of our country, is the younger generation experiencing a lower standard of living than their parents with more debt, lower wages and less home ownership?
Why, in the wealthiest country in the history of the world, do we have more people incarcerated than any other nation?
Why are we the only major country on earth not to guarantee health care to all, while we pay by far the highest prices in the world for prescription drugs?
Why will 500,000 Americans sleep out on the streets tonight while millions of others are paying 40 or 50 percent of their limited incomes for housing?
Why is there a gap in life expectancy of 15 years between the wealthiest Americans and the poorest, with life expectancy in our country actually in decline?
Why do we have more wealth and income inequality than any other major nation with the disparity between the rich and poor growing wider? Three individuals now own more wealth than the bottom half of America, the top one percent owns more wealth than the bottom 92 percent and 49 percent of all new income goes to the top one percent.
And here is the reason why.
Unfettered capitalism works extraordinarily well for the very rich and powerful special interests, while it is a disaster for ordinary Americans. While the billionaire class becomes richer, tens of millions of Americans are unable to afford the basic necessities of life – decent food, shelter, housing and education – and many are going deeper and deeper into debt.
**What differentiates our campaign from all others is the understanding that real change in this country will not occur unless we have the courage to build a political revolution – unless millions of people stand up and take on the greed of the powerful special interests that control so much of our economic and political life.**
We must not only win this election. We must build the kind of powerful grassroots movement that can bring transformative change to the country.
Together, we must finally confront the power structure of this country whose greed has done so much harm to the economic and moral fabric of America.
Our job is to take on Wall Street and break up the major financial institutions that have so much power over our economy. Our job is to take on the insurance industry and move this country to a Medicare for all single payer program. Our job is to take on the pharmaceutical industry and lower prescription drugs by half. Our job is to take on the fossil fuel industry and save the planet by transforming our energy system away from fossil fuels and into energy efficiency and sustainable energy. Our job is to take on the military industrial complex, cut military spending and end endless wars. Our job is to take on the prison industrial complex, end private prisons and mass incarceration. Our job, finally, is to bring about comprehensive immigration reform.
It goes without saying that what we are trying to do is enormously difficult. We are taking on the entire economic and political establishment. In that regard, I am reminded of the remarks that President Franklin D. Roosevelt made during his campaign in 1936 when he talked about the corporate elite of his time.
> _“Never before in all our history have these forces been so united against one candidate as they stand today. They are unanimous in their hate for me — and I welcome their hatred.”_
We are not going to run away from the power of corporate America. We will confront it and we will win.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
**Our campaign is approaching a critical FEC fundraising deadline at the end of this month.

Please make a donation today before the deadline comes to a close. If we stand together, we will win.**
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/12469?refcode=em190617-1-60&t=2&akid=1729%2E150750%2Ex9hSQK)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=1729%2E150750%2Ex9hSQK). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1729%2E150750%2Ex9hSQK). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=1729_150750_x9hSQK&akid=1729%2E150750%2Ex9hSQK).

Why do we have more wealth and income inequality than any other major nation with the disparity between the rich and poor growing wider? Three individuals now own more wealth than the bottom half of America, the top one percent owns more wealth than the bottom 92 percent and 49 percent of all new income goes to the top one percent. And here is the reason why. Unfettered capitalism works extraordinarily well for the very rich and powerful special interests, while it is a disaster for ordinary Americans. While the billionaire class becomes richer, tens of millions of Americans are unable to afford the basic necessities of life – decent food, shelter, housing and education – and many are going deeper and deeper into debt. What differentiates our campaign from all others is the understanding that real change in this country will not occur unless we have the courage to build a political revolution – unless millions of people stand up and take on the greed of the powerful special interests that control so much of our economic and political life. We must not only win this election. We must build the kind of powerful grassroots movement that can bring transformative change to the country. Together, we must finally confront the power structure of this country whose greed has done so much harm to the economic and moral fabric of America. Our job is to take on Wall Street and break up the major financial institutions that have so much power over our economy. Our job is to take on the insurance industry and move this country to a Medicare for all single payer program. Our job is to take on the pharmaceutical industry and lower prescription drugs by half. Our job is to take on the fossil fuel industry and save the planet by transforming our energy system away from fossil fuels and into energy efficiency and sustainable energy. Our job is to take on the military industrial complex, cut military spending and end endless wars. Our job is to take on the prison industrial complex, end private prisons and mass incarceration. Our job, finally, is to bring about comprehensive immigration reform. It goes without saying that what we are trying to do is enormously difficult. We are taking on the entire economic and political establishment. In that regard, I am reminded of the remarks that President Franklin D. Roosevelt made during his campaign in 1936 when he talked about the corporate elite of his time. “Never before in all our history have these forces been so united against one candidate as they stand today. They are unanimous in their hate for me — and I welcome their hatred.” We are not going to run away from the power of corporate America. We will confront it and we will win. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders Our campaign is approaching a critical FEC fundraising deadline at the end of this month. Please make a donation today before the deadline comes to a close. If we stand together, we will win. Paid for by Bernie 2020 PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

name: ElizabethWarren.com
subject: A little behind where we need to be -- can you chip in to help close the gap?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Elizabeth isn't going to fly from coast to coast to host a spree of swanky fundraising events, where you can only get in, meet her, and tell her what's on your mind if you can write a big fat check. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e18d/45c30650/16d4df345/669b064d/2216021155/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com")
---
On Team Warren, we _love_ having a plan.

Elizabeth has a plan to make Washington work for everyone, not just the wealthy and well-connected.

And we have a plan to make it happen: Keep growing our grassroots movement.

**The next step: Hitting our fundraising goal before the end of June.** That's the next FEC reporting deadline -- a chance for everyone to see how much we've raised and how many people have chipped in to own a piece of our movement.

**But we looked at the numbers, and we 're a little behind where we need to be. We've got to hit this goal to keep our ideas for big, structural change front and center in this race.

[We only ask when it really matters, and this is one of those moments: Will you make your first donation right now to help close the gap? $3 would make a real difference.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e18d/45c30650/16d4df345/669b064c/2216021155/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWp1bjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNjE3ZW0xLWZ1bGwtYiJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-jun19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190617em1-full-b")**

Every day, we keep an eye on whether we're still on track to hit the goals we set for major deadlines like June 30. And we'll need an increase in the next couple of days to get back on track.

But here's what we're _not_ going to do:

Elizabeth isn't going to fly from coast to coast to host a spree of swanky fundraising events, where you can only get in, meet her, and tell her what's on your mind if you can write a big fat check. She doesn't host _any_ events like that -- every supporter gets equal access.

We won't dial up deep-pocketed donors. We won't take a dime from any federal lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind. And we won't butter up any billionaires who could make up the difference by running a Super PAC.

Instead, here's the plan: We keep building this movement side by side.

**Grassroots donors are the only people who power this campaign, and this is one of the moments when we need all hands on deck.

[Please chip in for the first time right now to help make sure we're on track to hit our goal before the end of June.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e18d/45c30650/16d4df345/669b064c/2216021155/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWp1bjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNjE3ZW0xLWZ1bGwtYiJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-jun19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190617em1-full-b")**

Thanks for everything,

Team Warren

name: Wilmot Collins
subject: Join the fight to address climate change (add your name)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Friend,
We face an imminent environmental, social, and economic crisis unlike anything we've ever seen: climate change. As the next Senator from Montana, I will do everything in my power to address this threat.
Global climate change is undeniably a defining issue of our time. We must address and slow climate change with boldness and urgency. Our farmers are already feeling the devastating impacts, our recreation economy is being impacted, and our families' futures are at risk.
**[Together, we can rise to meet the climate challenge. Add your name to join the fight.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/5616476b/1e3760f0/69b0a8fb/5f7eaac3/3155223757/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/fight-to-address-climate-change?source=em190617&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
I'm asking you to believe in science and to be a part of the extraordinary efforts it will take to save our planet from climate catastrophe.
I'm asking you to help move us into the new clean energy economy and provide jobs for Montanans in energy efficiency, renewable energy, and environmental restoration.
I'm asking you to help protect the most vulnerable among us, from low-income Montanans who are unduly burdened by the rising costs associated with climate change to basic necessities like health care and food, to our farmers and ranchers who provide our food but face increased periods of drought and other natural disasters.
It's critical that we act now to address the threat climate change poses to our state, our country and our planet. We have too much to lose by continuing to ignore this important issue. And with an abundance of resources to provide, Montana has much to gain by transformation to a clean, reliable and resilient energy sector.
**[Stand with me in the urgent fight to address climate change. Sign the petition now.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/5616476b/1e3760f0/69b0a8fb/5f7eaac3/3155223757/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/fight-to-address-climate-change?source=em190617&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
Thank you,
Wilmot

name: Dan Baer
subject: I've said it before
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
Plenty of folks don't want people like us to make their voices heard. (Trust me -- they tell me so on Twitter and Facebook all the time!)
|
---

| | I've said it before: Of course my being gay isn't what makes me the most qualified candidate in this race. I'm running on my record of public service working for Colorado students and families here at home, and standing up for our values overseas. But my being gay does matter. It matters because it's part of my story and it helped teach me empathy. And it matters because _**breaking boundaries is one of the ways that we make other people 's stories possible.**_ Those who broke boundaries before me certainly helped make my story possible. [**So I 'm humbly asking for $19 and your help before midnight to help my campaign make history. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82600/232dfd1/95f91db/62a1748f/2181100240/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190617_em_fr _b1_baer_all&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on") I'm proud to have served under President Obama as one of the first openly gay U.S. Ambassadors. And I'll be proud to serve my home state of Colorado as the first openly gay man in the U.S. Senate if elected. We've come a long way, but plenty of folks don't want people like us to make their voices heard. (Trust me -- they tell me so on Twitter and Facebook all the time!) They spew hatred and bigotry. They fight against building a stronger, more inclusive America. But enlarging the circle of who counts, working to ensure that every American can enjoy life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness -- that's what gets me up each morning. That's why this campaign is so important to me. I want to stand up for those who have been left behind, no matter their background. An activist friend of mine in Denver tweeted recently "Love only wins if we fight for it." There's nothing automatic about progress. That's why I have a favor to ask of you: Can you chip in $19 or the cost of a couple beers as you're out celebrating Pride Month or enjoying a summer evening with friends? Will you help us change the conversation, bring big ideas to the Senate, and make history? Grassroots donations like yours are what truly power this campaign, and I couldn't be more grateful. | | [**Donate $19 now >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82600/232dfd1/95f91db/62a1748c/2181100240/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190617_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: a very important read about those who experience disabilities and voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
Justin Milliken is a non-verbal Granite Stater who experiences cerebral palsy and uses a wheelchair. He communicates largely through non-verbal cues and facial expressions.
Last November, with assistance from his mother, Justin was able to cast a ballot in the 2018 elections. More than six month's later, Justin's mother came under investigation for voter fraud.
Justin's mother has since been cleared, but let me say this: in New Hampshire, voters must be 18 years old, a U.S. citizen, and reside in the town in which they are voting. Every single voter has a right to get help if they can't mark a ballot on their own. I know because my son Ben experiences cerebral palsy just like Justin, and he receives help from one of his health care professionals at the polls.
Americans who experience disabilities may not always communicate the way others do. That doesn't mean they aren't cognitively aware and capable, and it certainly doesn't mean they that don't want to partake in democracy.
Justin's story is an important one -- you can read it in more detail [**here**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc221/2a380d31/3eeba8a1/f123c1/2597248728/VEsH/ "https://www.vnews.com/Officials-weigh-in-on-Charlestown-voting-dispute-26252416").
Thank you for listening.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan
>

_ _

# Once again Emmer puts politics above principle.

Last week Donald Trump said in a widely publicized interview that he would knowingly and willfully accept information on Democratic Presidential candidates from foreign governments. Shortly after, the FBI director and the chair of FEC put out statements saying unequivocally that such behavior would be a violation of the law. Even Senate Republicans such as Mitt Romney called this "simply unthinkable".

Later last week when asked directly Tom Emmer dodged the question and made it unclear whether or not he himself (as the chair of the NRCC) would refuse to accept stolen/hacked information. This is a very serious concern as our election security was already threatened in the 2016 election and existing vulnerabilities have yet to be seriously addressed in many places. The last thing we need is for our representatives to waffle on such a critical issue simply because it might be politically expedient.

Today I am asking you to [donate ](https://IanToddforcongress.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0824156d3c3076099692cab73&id=1b6f53617b&e=c56804a14d)to my campaign because every dollar we raise and every door we knock puts that much more pressure on Tom Emmer to give real answers and to stand up for the rule of law.

Your friend,

_Ian A. Todd_

Big league. **Today, the women of the Supreme Court came together with conservative Justices Gorsuch and Thomas to ensure that the newly drawn map stays in place -- a huge win for voting rights in a key swing state. ** [**We 're grateful to the Supreme Court for delivering this major win for enfranchisement. name: Shepard Nevel, Team Hickenlooper
subject: [ADD YOUR NAME] Fighting climate change will take true collaboration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
| | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf9f/3811083867/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, I'm Shepard, I'm on the policy team here at Team Hickenlooper, and we just helped John roll out his plan to combat climate change. It's bold, unique, and I can't wait to tell you more--but first, will you stand with John to amplify his voice on this issue? **Climate change is the defining challenge of our time, and we need a bold, nationwide mobilization plan to avert the consequences to our planet. Add your name now if you agree that our next president needs to make this a priority.** [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf9c/3811083867/VEsE/ "http://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/make-the-climate-crisis-a-priority?source=em_20190617") A lot of ideas to fight climate change have been making the headlines lately, including the soundbite-friendly "Green New Deal." But John refuses to campaign on an easy fix or on promises he can't execute _on_. As he likes to say, great ideas are a dime a dozen: what matters is what you do. And with the clock running out for our planet, nothing matters more than what we can actually get done in Washington. **John 's the only candidate in this primary who has actually brought people--often people on polar opposite ends of the spectrum--together to pursue bold, concrete solutions to protect our planet. Having done it in Colorado, he's ready to do it for our nation.** Fundamental to John's approach is _collaboration_. We're not going to solve the climate crisis with massive expansions of government, or with extreme rhetoric that triggers backlash from our opponents and costs us even more valuable time to take action. Just as an example: As Governor of Colorado, by taking the opposite tack and bringing opposing parties to the table, John passed the strictest methane regulations in the country--_and_ got the oil and gas industry to pay for it. We have to work together to fight this crisis: Democrats, Republicans, government, the private sector, environmental activists, the energy industry, developing and developed nations alike. It starts with:
* **Rejoining the Paris Agreement,** re-forging our relationships with our global climate partners, and building climate goals into trade agreements and even foreign aid and military agreements
* **Treating the private sector as indispensable partners for climate action** and boldly unleashing market forces to discover the technological and infrastructural solutions we need
* **Putting a scientific approach first:** Solving this crisis will take listening to and learning from the best minds from every corner of the world. As the only trained scientist in the primary, John will make sure all climate change efforts are based on the best available data and worldwide scientific consensus.

[_**Want to get into the nitty-gritty details? Read John 's full plan here, and don't forget to add your name below.**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf9d/3811083867/VEsF/ "https://medium.com/@johnhickenlooper/governor-hickenloopers-bold-global-plan-to-combat-climate-change-ab47df798792") [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf9a/3811083867/VEsC/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/make-the-climate-crisis-a-priority?source=em_20190617") We can't wait to share more of John's policies with you as they're rolled out. Stay tuned. Thanks, Shepard Shepard Nevel
Senior Policy Advisor
Hickenlooper 2020
[](https://secure.hickenlooper. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_pet_20190617_SN_20190617-pet-SN-climate-all_all_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf99/3811083867/VEsB/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf96/3811083867/VEsO/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf97/3811083867/VEsP/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf94/3811083867/VEsHBQ/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").
We love staying connected to you--but if at any point you'd like to opt out, you can do so right [here](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637717d7/691c9bd/6df5208b/11a4cf95/3811083867/VEsHBA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").
